learning_outcomes.description: Identify and apply analytics across a range of emerging business domains.
Evaluate appropriate analytic techniques that can be used within different applied business domains.
Communicate analytic insights effectively to diverse stakeholders.

assessments.assessment_title: A1: Design a Continuous Auditing Model
assessments.task_description: The task in this assessment is to design, describe and apply a continuous auditing model, based on your reading and understanding of the details presented in the DBS Bank case study “Innovation in Assurance: Doing More, and More Effectively, with Less”.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: A2: Design a Machine Learning Model
assessments.task_description:
In this assignment, students have the option to design and develop a new machine learning model or utilise and adapt the model that was previously recommended in module 2.2.

The key task is to choose and endorse just one model, providing justification for why it is the recommended model with respect to three critical areas of healthcare analytics: operational management, cost management, and care management.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.assessment_title: A3: HR Data Analysis and Report
assessments.task_description:
The key task in this assessment is to provide insights into the features that explains or may be associated with turnover patterns of +10,000 strong group of employees using the data set provided.

You will need to identify an interesting finding within your analytics and develop a 1000-word (maximum) report on findings.
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

assessments.task_description: For this assessment, you will need to write an essay reflecting on your learning experience across this course, applying the four components of reflective thinking, adapted from The Integrated Reflective Cycle (Bassot, 2013).
assessments.deferral_or_extension: You may be able to apply for an extension.
assessments.late_submission: A penalty of 10% of the maximum possible mark will be deducted per 24 hours from time submission is due for up to 7 days. After 7 days, you will receive a mark of 0.
assessments.additional_info: You may be able to apply for an extension.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Audit Analytics

Self-Directed Learning Sub-Module 1.1: Foundations of Audit Analytics. This topic identifies and explains the key principles of audit and assurance, explores the purposes of data analytics in the field of audit, and introduces the audit decision-making framework.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1: Audit Analytics Self-Directed Learning Sub-Module 1.1: Foundations of Audit Analytics. This topic identifies and explains the key principles of audit and assurance, explores the purposes of data analytics in the field of audit, and introduces the audit decision-making framework.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Audit Analytics

Self-Directed Learning Sub-Module 1.2: Audit Analytic Techniques. This topic focuses on the alignment between analytic tools and techniques, and the audit engagement.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03
learning_activities.topic_description: Module 1: Audit Analytics Self-Directed Learning Sub-Module 1.2: Audit Analytic Techniques. This topic focuses on the alignment between analytic tools and techniques, and the audit engagement.

learning_activities.topic:
Module 1: Audit Analytics

Self-Directed Learning Sub-Module 1.3 : Machine Learning and AI in the Audit Domain. This topic explores Machine Learning (ML) and Artificial Intelligence (AI) in the field of audit analytics, placing specific focus on the Continuous Auditing model.


Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.topic:
Module 2: Healthcare Analytics

Self-Directed Learning: Transformation in the Healthcare Domain. This topic focuses on the application of data analysis techniques in healthcare. You will explore the Healthcare Analytics Framework and review a variety of case studies featuring the application of bespoke analytics to solve healthcare related challenges.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02

learning_activities.topic:
Module 3: HR Analytics

Learning outcomes: L01, L02, L03

Module 3: HR Analytics

Self-Directed Learning: The Three Core Pillars of HR Analytics. This topic introduces the concepts of the Human Resources (HR) function, Human Resource Management (HRM) and the three pillars of HR Analytics. The first pillar of HR Analytics, Engagement, is explored in detail.

Learning outcomes: L01, L02

Module 3: HR Analytics

Self-Directed Learning: Employee Performance. This topic explores the second core pillar of HR analytics, employee performance. An industry case snapshot is also provided, which will play a central role in your third assessment task.
